content:
Matthew Stanley is a Mechanical Engineer with a focus in system controls, object detection, and machine learning. He received his undergraduate degree at Gonzaga University in the Fall of 2018. He is expected to obtain his masters degree from Colorado School of Mines in the Fall of 2019.
His most recent works involve consulting work at Harbor Electronics and Vigilant Solutions. Matthew is working on the DARPA Subt. Challenge for the Colorado School of Mines team with a focus on the object detection algorithm.
summary:
Matthew Stanley is a Mechanical Engineer with a focus in system controls, object detection, and machine learning. He received his undergraduate degree at Gonzaga University in the Fall of 2018. He is expected to obtain his masters degree from Colorado School of Mines in the Fall of 2019.
His most recent works involve consulting work at Harbor Electronics and Vigilant Solutions. Matthew is working on the DARPA Subt. Challenge for the Colorado School of Mines team with a focus on the object detection algorithm.
title: Matthew Stanley

content:
For our final project in Mechatronics, we created a claw that would grab an object and calculate the width of it. I worked on this project with Christian Crowley and Yunsik Jung.
The criteria for this project was to create a device with at least one sensor and one actuator. It also needed to be programmable and use a custom designed pcb.
summary: The aim of this project is to create a claw that knows the size of the object it is grasping.
title: Claw Arm

content:
For our final project in Human Centered Robotics, we investigated different exploration methods for mapping an indoor environment. I performed this project with Alex Dodge, Mitchell Waibel, and Tyler Thune.
The equipment utilized for this project were a TurtleBot 2 with and Orbbec Astra camera. For mapping the Turtlebot’s surroundings, we compared the Real-Time Apperance Based Mapping and GMapping algorithms. For exploration, we used the Fronteir Exploration algorithm.
summary: The aim of this project is to have a turtlebot explore and map its environment.
title: Turtlebot

summary: Our research investigated the Poisson's ratio of the Hummingbird Hawkmoth across the surface of the wing.
title: Effects of Uniaxial Tensile Strain on Moth Wings Through Digital Image Correlation
